entry.title: Electric Mobility A Shield Against Global Volatility
entry.summary: In the 1970s South Africans faced strict fuel rationing, 80km/h speed limits, and dry weekends where not a drop of petrol could be bought. Conflict in the Middle-East back then was a stark lesson in how vulnerable a nation becomes when its transport and logistics…

entry.title: The case for EVs runs deeper than fuel price
entry.summary: Every time fuel prices rise, the conversation around electric vehicles picks up. Search trends spike, showroom enquiries increase, and suddenly everyone has an opinion on whether now is the right time to make the switch. It is a familiar cycle. What is different this time,…

entry.title: SA’s Automotive Future Hinges on Local Battery Manufacturing
entry.summary: A new World Bank report suggests that battery manufacturing and local beneficiation of battery minerals will determine whether South Africa can preserve its automotive base and build new industrial capability. The country’s vehicle production for export has long depended on Europe and the United Kingdom.

entry.title: Why SA Imports Battery Cells
entry.summary: South Africa is the world's number one producer of manganese, outputting around 7.4 million tonnes in 2024. This is roughly the same amount as Gabon and Australia combined. The country also holds significant vanadium reserves, with Bushveld Vanadium operating one of the most advanced vanadium electrolyte…

entry.title: Off-Grid Ambition Meets the Reality of Fleet Electrification
entry.summary: South Africa's fleet electrification conversation has, for several years, been dominated by questions about vehicle availability and purchase cost.  Less examined is the infrastructure problem sitting underneath both of those concerns. Even if fleet operators were ready to electrify tomorrow, the national electricity grid almost…

entry.title: Petrol, diesel or PHEV: which makes financial sense?
entry.summary: Global oil markets have been unsettled by ongoing tensions between Iran and the United States, and the effects are reaching South African forecourts. The threat to supply routes through the Strait of Hormuz has kept energy traders on edge, and that pressure is now translating…

entry.title: CNBM and Geely SA Drive Clean Energy Forward
entry.summary: China National Building Materials (CNBM) South Africa and Geely Auto South Africa have signed a Strategic Cooperation Framework Agreement to work together on clean energy and electric mobility projects across South Africa and the wider Southern African region. The agreement was signed at the Future…

entry.title: Why EVs Are Expensive in South Africa
entry.summary: You've probably looked at an electric vehicle (EV) price tag and thought: how is this thing worth that much? Especially when you know that charging one costs a fraction of what you'd spend on petrol. It's a fair question. And the answer, bluntly, is tax.

entry.title: Chery Outlines NEV Strategy
entry.summary: Tony Liu, the head of Chery Group in South Africa, gave a talk at the NADAConnect 2026 Conference about the company’s future plans. In his presentation, Liu showed off several car brands, including Omoda, Jaecoo, and Lepas. He explained that the company is moving away…
